title: Issues with getting composefs storage option to work in WSL2
body:
### Issue Description

I have tried to get composefs storage option to work in podman running in WSL for some time, tried multiple times (environmentally from scratch) over the last 9 months, last try yesterday.

The current state is that I know that composefs in itself works and that the composefs related options are passed to podman are actually found in logs when doing a pull also, but still not been able to verify that it actually ends up in storage as expected.

I have tried to get help in the podman discord and the composefs github but still without being able to reach the end goal of a successful verification of it working.

Link to composefs issue: https://github.com/composefs/composefs/discussions/398
Link to two most recent posts in podman discord: 
https://discord.com/channels/852634929845239818/852634929845239824/1405954990216052818
https://discord.com/channels/852634929845239818/852634929845239824/1338544475462439044

The resources utilized related to how to enable and verify the composefs support are:
Related to config: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b1a32f65eed2771f2b8e865babcaf466d86c79a8/contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh#L61
https://gist.github.com/cgwalters/f3851137d2855500ca39740a4ffed044
Config, but maybe more on expected file system layout: https://archive.fosdem.org/2024/events/attachments/fosdem-2024-3250-composefs-and-containers/slides/22189/ComposeFS_fosdem_pb16gQM.pdf


### Steps to reproduce the issue

NOTE: I have tried with other older versions of wsl, ubuntu and podman in previous attempts but with same results.

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install a recent ubuntu(24.04) wsl2 distro
2. Build erofs kernel module from source, build composefs(1.0.8) from source, build podman(5.7.0-dev) from source
3. (Configure storage.conf according to sources linked above): Step 4 was was what actually seemed to have any effect related to composefs in the logs
4. Invoke podman as root with:  sudo ./bin/podman --log-level=trace --storage-opt overlay.use_composefs=true --pull-option enable_partial_images=true --pull-option convert_images=true pull alpine:latest
5. Evaluate log files, search for composefs/zstd/erofs/chunked...
6. Evaluate folder structure and contents of **/var/lib/containers/storage/**


### Describe the results you received

Evaluate log files, search for composefs/zstd/erofs/chunked...:
```
[time="2025-08-15T18:45:41+02:00" level=debug msg="Called pull.PersistentPreRunE(./bin/podman --log-level=trace --storage-opt overlay.use_composefs=true --pull-option enable_partial_images=true --pull-option convert_images=true pull alpine:latest)"

time="2025-08-15T18:45:41+02:00" level=debug msg="reference \"[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.use_composefs=true]docker.io/library/alpine:latest\" does not resolve to an image ID"
time="2025-08-15T18:45:41+02:00" level=debug msg="reference \"[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.use_composefs=true]localhost/alpine:latest\" does not resolve to an image ID"
time="2025-08-15T18:45:41+02:00" level=debug msg="reference \"[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.use_composefs=true]docker.io/library/alpine:latest\" does not resolve to an image ID"
time="2025-08-15T18:45:41+02:00" level=debug msg="parsed reference into \"[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.use_composefs=true]docker.io/library/alpine:latest\""
time="2025-08-15T18:45:41+02:00" level=debug msg="Copying source image //alpine:latest to destination image [overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.use_composefs=true]docker.io/library/alpine:latest"

time="2025-08-15T18:45:43+02:00" level=debug msg="parsed reference into \"[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.use_composefs=true]@9234e8fb04c47cfe0f49931e4ac7eb76fa904e33b7f8576aec0501c085f02516\""
time="2025-08-15T18:45:43+02:00" level=debug msg="Called pull.PersistentPostRunE(./bin/podman --log-level=trace --storage-opt overlay.use_composefs=true --pull-option enable_partial_images=true --pull-option convert_images=true pull alpine:latest)"Describe the results you received](url)
```

comments.body:
I'm now pretty confident I have been able to successfully verify (repeatable) that it works in one out of two scenarios.
The scenario that works is when pulling from a repository, what doesn't work is when I'm building images locally.

My test case consists of generating 5 images, with a shared base image (ubuntu 22.04, 80mb), to which I then add a generated random contents file of size 20M and copy into a second layer, I then add a single small unique text file to a third layer and then finally squash them, with the end result of a 2 layer image(base image + second layer consisting of the shared 20M file + a small unique file). Running `du -sh` on the `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay` shows 182M _(~80M+(5*~20M)=~180M)_, the first layer is shared because it has the same hash, the rest does not get shared even thou I had expected them to be with the use of composefs and it's file level deduplication support. Basic overlay storage will not be able to share this second layer between the images because of the unique file (as expected).

After this I push them to a registry and then clean up the local cache via `podman rmi --all -f` and also restart podman using: `podman system reset --force` and then verify the local storage disk usage is zero.

Finally I pull the 5 pushed images from the registry and then check the used disk space once again in `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay`, which now shows 102M, _(~80M + ~20M = ~100M)_, the 2M differ in both examples seems like the metadata overhead of 5 images.

**Question:** Is it a bug that composefs does not work with local image builds or is it just a feature not yet implemented?

@KristoferHansson, your last comment says that composefs is used when pulling images, but not when building them locally. In the issue description, you failed to use composefs even when pulling the images: has something changed, and you forgot to update the issue description? And you use an ad-hoc WSL instance (Ubuntu-based). Have you tried with the default WSL podman instance (Fedora-based)? cc @giuseppe

> **Question:** Is it a bug that composefs does not work with local image builds or is it just a feature not yet implemented?

the composefs image is created at pull time, so it doesn't work for locally built images.  We've not thought about that case though, we would need to convert regular layers anyway.
